entry.title: Enhance MB21
entry.content:
When creating a Reservation to transfer materials from one Plant to another, I want the WBS Element field to be positioned next to materials, so that each Material has its own corresponding WBS Element in the same row.

Currently, the WBS Element appears as a single field at the top, but I need it to be linked to each Material individually in the same row.

How can I achieve this?

entry.title: Re: Transportation Management : Not used freight units
entry.content:
Hello @Peter2408 

If some of the shipments are handled without TM, then they should not be integrated with TM. That can be customized in SPRO: Transportation Management -> Integration -> Logistics Integration -> ...

If the standard configuration is not flexible enough, you can use the BADI_LO_TM_SLS, BADI_LO_TM_SHP BAdIs to implement custom integration logic.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: BRF+ Output Management

Hi,

Good insight but have one question,

Does BRF+ for output management allow flexibility of maintaining output channel per supplier? Meaning...vendor A is on Email, Vendor B is on Print and Vendor C is on EDI...

It was easily possible in NAST based output management.

entry.title: PR release cancellation - hierarchy
entry.content:
Hi Gurus, 

Could you please advise if it is possible to cancel PR release in the reversal order? The PR is approved By the approver 1 and approver 2 (the approver 1 approves first)/ But in case we need to cancel the approval it can be un-approved in any order by any approver. What business wants is that the PR to be un-approved by approver 2 first, and then - by the approver 1. Other order is unacceptable. 

I tried to play with settings on header and item level but they do not have any effect on the order of approval cancellation..

entry.title: Re: how to restrict serial numbers at plant level in ewm
entry.content: Use stock based serialization (type C). The stock has the entitled (business partner of the plant) in its business key. The serial numbers are assigned to the stock. During picking you can only choose serial numbers for this stock, so it is definitely the correct plant.

entry.title: Re: Manage outbound deliveries can't select a batch autom...
entry.content:
Hello,

The issue you are experiencing with the "Manage Outbound Deliveries" app where it incorrectly states that a delivery contains a batch, when no batch is assigned, might be related to the app's handling of batch determination or data validation rules.

Here are some steps you might consider to resolve this issue:

1. **Check Configuration for Batch Management**: Make sure that the configuration for batch management is correctly set up in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system. This includes ensuring that the materials involved are flagged for batch management and that batch determination is active.

2. **Batch Determination Strategy**: Ensure that a batch determination strategy is maintained for the material in question. Go to the batch determination strategies in the system to confirm that the strategy is set up correctly for the warehouse process.

3. **Check for Updates or Notes**: Review if there are any updates or SAP Notes about the "Manage Outbound Deliveries" app (App ID: F0233A) that address this specific issue. This might include bug fixes or patches that correct this behavior.

4. **Verify Data Sources**: Confirm that the data sources or integrations pulling data into the "Manage Outbound Deliveries" app are configured correctly and do not have any conflicts or outdated information that might cause this error.

5. **Testing in an Isolated Environment**: Try replicating the error in a testing environment to ensure that it is not related to a specific configuration or custom development in your live environment.

Here are the steps and considerations for setting up automatic batch determination:

1. **Material Master Data Configuration**:
- Verify that the material for which you want to perform batch determination is correctly configured in the material master. Ensure that the Batch Management indicator is set.

2. **Batch Determination Procedure**:
- Establish a batch determination procedure in your system, which includes all necessary condition tables, access sequences, and strategy types.

3. **Condition Records for Batch Determination**:
- Maintain condition records for batch determination. This includes defining rules and conditions under which the batch determination occurs during outbound delivery processing.

4. **Configuration in the Outbound Process**:
- Configure the outbound delivery processes to incorporate batch determination. This often involves configuring the delivery item category to trigger automatic batch determination.

5. **Automatic Batch Determination Settings**:
- Ensure that the setting for automatic batch determination is enabled in the relevant delivery document type and warehouse management processes. This might require adjustments in the Customizing settings under Logistics Execution.

6. **Use of Batch Determination in the App**:
- When using the "Manage Outbound Deliveries" app, ensure that your process flow includes steps that trigger batch determination based on your configurations. Check for any user-specific settings that might affect batch assignments.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Unlocking Efficiency: Leveraging SAP BTP Document Management Service in Fiori Applications # 3
entry.content:
INTRODUCTION:

In today's digital landscape,efficient document management is crucial for businesses aiming to streamline operations and enhance productivity.The SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Document Management Service offers a robust solution for managing documents seamlessly within SAP Fiori applications.
In this blog series we will explore different scenarios and find suitable solutions to integrate DMS into SAP

Fiori Applications. This will help consultants to
Blog # 1: Embed SAP BTP Attachment Reuse UI in a Custom Fiori App 
Blog # 2: Embed SAP BTP Attachment Reuse UI in an SAP Fiori Elements application 
Blog # 3: Using SAP BTP DMS in an SAP Business Process Automation and integrate to a Task UI application
Blog # 4: Using SAP BTP DMS API for Custom requirements.

In the previous blog we covered using SAP BTP Attachment Reuse UI in SAP Fiori Elements applications.

In the 3rd installment of the series, we will cover using SAP BTP Attachment Reuse UI in an approval scenario in My Inbox App using Task UI.We will use a simple SAP Build Process Automation(SBPA) Workflow for the purpose.

Prerequisite

1. Set up a Dev Space in Business Application Studio
2. Add Entitlement for following services in your sub account
   a. SAP BTP Document Management Service, Integration Option – Standard (Instance)
   b. SAP BTP Document Management Service, Application Option – Standard (Subscription)
3. Cloud Foundry
4. Set up SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA)
5. Set up SAP Build Workzone, Standard-Edition

Setting Up SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Workzone

SAP Build Process Automation in the SAP Business Technology Platform combines the capabilities of SAP Workflow Management and SAP Intelligent RPA in an easy-to-use, no-code AI-powered experience. It increases organizations’ capacity to drive process automation by empowering business experts to become citizen developers. Leveraging the power of no-code, citizen developers can build, adapt, improve, and innovate business processes with minimum assistance from scarce IT resources.

To access the Applications provided by SBPA, we need to integrate it with SAP Build Workzone.

Follow the tutorial https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-configure-workzone.html   to do the setup and integration

Follow the tutorial https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-subscribe-booster.html   to set up SBPA in your trial or paid subaccount.

Setting Up Document Management Service and Integrate with SBPA

We have set up SAP BTP DMS in the previous blog

Note down the Repository ID. This will be used in the current blog. If you are using DMS Application option, the Root ID and Folder ID can be retrieved form directly the url. I will be in format of
https://<sub-account-url>/cp.portal/site#user-display?sap-ui-app-id-hint=com.sap.ecm.sdm.user&/documents/v1/open/rep=<RepositoryID>&obj=<ObjectID>

You may also use DMS API to retrieve the details. Please check the link shared earlier.

The next step is to establish connection between SBPA and SAP BTP DMS Service.

The configuration steps are explained on SAP Help https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/configure-attachment-destination-in-sap-btp-subaccount

Follow the instructions to create a destination “sap_process_automation_document_store” in you BTP subaccount and then refer it in SBPA control tower.

Scenario

We are taking a very simple scenario in this blog.A user triggers a Approval process for a Material, which an approver reviews and approves.The trigger form has a simple interface for user to add few basic details and add attachments.

The Approval task will have the details provided by the user along with SAP BTP.

PART#1 : Create a TASK UI Fiori App

The general information on how to create a Task UI for Workflow Approval process is provided at https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-create-sapui5-task-orderapproval.html  

In current blog we will restrict ourselves to the changes that are required to integrate DMS to this application.

Step 1: Create a new Task UI app using the referenced tutorial.

Step 2: Modify the manifest to update the import/export parameters required for the integration to SBPA workflow.When an Attachment object is added to trigger UI, the folder (Object ID) is passed on the SBPA runtime as a string property. This property is of main importance for us for DMS integration.

 

"sap.bpa.task": {
        "_version": "1.0.0",
        "outcomes": [
            {
                "id": "approve",
                "label": "Approve"
            },
            {
                "id": "reject",
                "label": "Reject"
            }
        ],
        "inputs": {
            "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema",
            "title": "input",
            "type": "object",
            "required": [],
            "properties": {
                "Material": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "title": "Material",
                    "description": "Material"
                },
                "Country": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "title": "Country",
                    "description": "Country"
                },
                "MaterialType": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "title": "Material Type",
                    "description": "Material Type"
                },
                "NotesHistory": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "title": "Notes History",
                    "description": "Notes History"
                },
                "AttachmentFolder": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "title": "Attachment Folder",
                    "description": "Attachment Folder"
                }
            }
        },
        "outputs": {
            "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema",
            "title": "output",
            "type": "object",
            "required": [
                "NewNote"
            ],
            "properties": {
                "NewNote": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "title": "New Note",
                    "description": "New Note"
                }
            }
        },
  }

 

Step 3: Update manifest file to use the Reuse UI.

Add the Component usage following under “sap.ui5” section. Repository ID can also be added as a parameter as environment parameter in workflow for each environment and passed on to Task UI along with other parameters.

 

 

"componentUsages": {
            "documentTable": {
                "name": "com.sap.ecm.reuse.documentTable",
                "settings": {
                    "destinationPath": "/comsapecmreuse.comsapecmreusedocumentTable/api",
                    "repositoryId": "<Provide Repository ID>"
                }
            }
        },

 

After Component Usage, add the resource roots attributes.

 

"resourceRoots": {
            "com.sap.ecm.reuse.documentTable": "./../../comsapecmreuse.comsapecmreusedocumentTable/",
            "com.sap.ecm.reuse.admin": "./../comsapecmreuse.comsapecmreuseadmin/"
        }

 

 

The standard Reuse UI comes in 2 variants “documentTable" and “admin”. We are going to use “documentTable” variant for this blog series.

Step 4: Add the component in the xml view

 

<core:ComponentContainer id="sdi-document" usage="documentTable" async="false" manifest="true" componentCreated="onComponentCreated"/>

 

Step 5: Read the Object ID (Folder where SBPA Trigger UI has uploaded documents) to the component settings in control file. For each instance of Workflow this is unique folder that gets generated.

Please note that the attachment ID property has prefix “spa-res:cmis:folderid: ” which needs to be removed to get the real folder (or object ID). You can do it in controller or use script task in workflow to do the conversion.

 

onComponentCreated: function(oEvent){
   let oComp = oEvent.getSource().getComponentInstance();
   let oContextModel = this.getView().getModel("context");
   oContextModel.dataLoaded().then(function(oData){
   let sAttachmentPath = oContextModel.getProperty("/AttachmentFolder")?.replace("spa-res:cmis:folderid:","");
     oComp.setHomeFolderId(sAttachmentPath);
     oComp.requestNavigationAndOpen(oComp.getRepositoryId(), sAttachmentPath);
   }.bind(this));
}

 

Step 6: Update xs-app.json file to include route for DMS Integration API.

 

{
   "source": "^/comsapecmreuse.comsapecmreusedocumentTable/api(.*)$",
   "target": "$1",
   "authenticationType": "xsuaa",
   "service":"com.sap.ecm.reuse",
   "endpoint": "ecmservice"
}

 

Step 7: 

Update MTA file to enable authentication and authorization using client credential between Fiori Application and DMS service. Add Document Management Service, Integration option as a module.

 

- name: test-dms
    parameters:
      service-key: 
        name: sdm-key

 

Add destination configuration for DMS instance using the client credentials.

 

- Name: test-dms
   Authentication: OAuth2UserTokenExchange
   ServiceInstanceName: test-dms
   ServiceKeyName: sdm-key

 

 

Finally, add the DMS instance under resources section.

 

- name: test-dms
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    service: sdm
    service-name: test-dms
    service-plan: standard

 

Step 8: Deploy your app in cloud Foundry environment. You may choose to use context menu in project or use terminal commands (mbt build & cf deploy)

Step 9: Log-in to SAP BTP Cockpit and find the deployed application under „HTML Applications“ section.

We can’t test the application here as it needs to be embedded into an Approval step in SBPA workflow and then accessed through My Inbox.

PART 2 # : Create an SBPA Workflow

To add a task UI in an SBPA workflow , follow the tutorial @ https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-integrate-sapui5-task-process.html  In this section we will cover the most important steps only. 

Prerequisite:

Create a Workflow and add a Trigger Form to start the workflow. Add input fields and an attachment section.

Step 1: In your process overview , import a form.

Provide App ID and version and import the form.
Refer to manifest file to get the App ID

Confirm the Form has been imported.

Step 2: Open the workflow and add a script task. Format the folder ID.

Step 3: Add a new Approval step and select the Approval Form imported earlier.

 

Map the input parameters. Please note the attachmentFolderPath is used from custom task created in previous step.

Step 4: Release and Deploy the application.

TESTING : 

Step 1: Use the Form link from start event of the Deployed Workflow to trigger a new approval process.
Make sure an attachment is uploaded.

Step 2: Open My Inbox app on your workzone site. Please note using locally embedded "My Inbox" app in SBPA will not work due to approuter limitations.

Access the approval task item to validate the Task UI is embedded and hast he detils along with SAP BTP DMS Reuse UI.

CONCLUSION:  We learned how to embed the standard SAP BTP DMS Reuse UI in a SBPA Approval process using a Task UI.In the final blog of the series we will see how SAP BTP DMS API can be used in CAP based applications for custom scenarios.

entry.title: SuccessFactors – Variable Pay – Bonus Plan with variable Individual percentage
entry.content:
Business case

In most clients’ requirements for bonus calculation, we would find that the target bonus of employees is split between the Business part and the Individual part fairly consistently across the population. For instance, lower level employees would get 20% of their bonus from Business Goals and the other 80% from their Individual Goals, while higher level employees would get 70% of their bonus from their Business Goals and the other 30% from their Individual Goals.

However, in our business case, the client has multiple bonus plans, which represent the target percentage of the Variable Pay bonus, but this target can be split between Business and Individual parts differently according to the job code, department or location of the employee, or a combination of those fields. In order to minimise the number of Bonus plans we needed to define, we came up with a solution involving custom fields that calculate, based on the target % for the employee and the weight of their business goals, the remaining target to be assigned to individual goals.

I am sure there were other, and maybe better, solutions for this, but this blog might help others to think differently about the Variable Pay configuration for “flexible” bonus plans.

Illustration

To illustrate this, let’s take 2 employees who have a 15% target bonus and work in the same company but in 2 different departments.

Employee A works in Department X, and Employee B works in department Y. The bonus plans should look like the following:

Employee A15% Employee B15%
Business Goals10% Business Goals12%
Company Goal5% Company Goal5%
Department X Goal 12.5% Department Y Goal 13%
Department X Goal 22.5% Department Y Goal 22%
   Department Y Goal 32%
Individual Goals5% Individual Goals3%
Individual Goal 12% Individual Goal 11.5%
Individual Goal 23% Individual Goal 21.5%

Note that here the weights of the goals represent the portion of the salary assigned to the goal, not the portion of the bonus target as they usually would.

So, in the Worksheet, the entry for Employee B would look like the screenshot below:

 

Apart from its flexibility, the solution offers higher legibility of the details of the bonus calculation for the employees. It is easier to understand what each goal represents in terms of value and understand how the total value of the payout is calculated.

In the next section, details are given for the steps taken during the configuration of the solution. Apologies in advance for the length and the boredom you might feel by the end of it, but i guess there is no way to make this kind of thing fun. Nonetheless, I hope this helps or inspire someone when faced with unusual requirements from their customer. And Variable Pay implementations are a good place to find such unusual requirements! 

Configuration

To achieve what is shown in the screenshot above, we have in our set up for Variable pay, the following key elements:

  • The basis for the bonus is the salary, not the bonus target
  • Business Goals are assigned to bonus plan with the percentage representing the portion of the salary that Is their target
  • A number of custom fields are used to calculate the correct weight for Individual Goals

1. Bonus Calculation Set Up

The basis for the bonus is defined as the salary, not as the target bonus as would happen in most configurations

 

2. Bonus Plans Set Up

Business Goals

The Business Goals are defined as usual. Note that the Company business Goal is assigned to the program plan name so that all employees who have this goal in their bonus plan get the goal, no matter what data is in their Employee History

Bonus Plan

A single Bonus plan “Up to 15% Bonus” is defined, with the weights of the Individual and Business Section being 100% (so that the basis amount for the section is the 100% salary).

The business goals are then assigned with the weight percentage that correspond to the portion of the salary the employee would get if the goal was achieved at 100%

 

Worksheet Configuration

A number of custom columns are defined in the worksheet, at various levels, and most standard columns are hidden

Business Goal Level – Business Goal Weight

At Business Goal Level, we defined a column that gets the weight of the business goal as set in the bonus plan, in a Percent format, so that it can be used in other levels of the worksheet

Business Goal Level – Business Goal Payout Amount

At Business Goal Level, we defined a column that retrieves the goal payout amount so that it can be used in other levels of the worksheet

Business Section Level – Business Section Weight

At Business Section Level, we defined a column that sums up the business goals weights to determine the total weight of the business section

Business Section Level – Business Section Target Amount

At Business Section Level, we defined a column that calculates what the target amount is for the business section (to be used for display purposes, instead of the standard column that would show a wrong number)

Business Section Level – Business Section Payout Amount

At Business Section Level, we defined a column that sums up the goals payout amounts (to be used for display purposes, instead of the standard column that would show a wrong number)

Assignment Level – Business Section Weight

At Assignment Level, we defined a field that retrieves the weight of the Business Section (to be used in Individual Section Level to determine the weight of the individual section)

Individual Section Level – Total Bonus target Percentage

At Individual Section Level, we defined a field that reads the bonus target percentage of the employee in their Employee History File

Individual Section Level – Individual Section Weight

At Individual Section Level, we defined a field that calculates the weight to be assigned to the business section. This will then be used at Individual Goal level to determine the weight of each goal

Individual Detail Level – Individual Goal Weight

At Individual Detail Level, we defined a field that calculates the weight of each individual goal. This works by reading the weight of the goal that comes from the Individual Goal Plan of the employee, and multiplying this value by the weight if the Individual Section. Note that the Individual Goal Plan is configured to enforce the sum of all goal weights to add up to 100%, so that managers do not have to think about what percentage an employee is entitled to in their Individual bonus, they only have to assign the goals and spread the wights according to goal priority, the Variable Pay calculation will do the rest for them.

Individual Detail Level – Individual Goal Target Amount

At Individual Detail Level, we defined a field that calculates the target amount for each Individual Goal

Individual Detail Level – Individual Goal Payout Percentage

At Individual Detail Level, we defined a field that calculates the payout percentage for each Individual Goal. This is done so that unrated goals are paid out at 0%

Individual Detail Level – Individual Goal Payout Amount

At Individual Detail Level, we defined a field that calculates the payout amount for each Individual Goal.

Assignment Level – Individual Section Payout Amount

At Assignment level, we defined a field that retrieves the payout amount for the Individual Section (to be used at entry level, since the standard field does not calculate as needed)

Assignment Level – Individual Section Payout Target

At Assignment level, we defined a field that retrieves the target amount for the Individual Section (to be used at entry level, since the standard field does not calculate as needed)

entry.title: Re: Costs & Revenues getting mapped against separate WBS...
entry.content: Hello, have you consider using the WBS hierarchy in the Project Profitability report, this way the cost on 1.1 would roll up to 0.1, and 1.2 would roll up to 0.2 etc. Right click on the WBS and select the hierarchy and see if this helps.

entry.title: Re: SAP EBS: Question on Interpretation Algorithm "20 020...
entry.content:
Hi Vassili, 

@vassili_kromm 

I'm afraid I can't really help you with your actual question. 

However, after reading your question, I think it's important to inform you that the posting rules are a rather outdated feature. In an upcoming release in the (far) future, they are going to be deprecated and removed from the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system.
Instead of the posting rules, I'd recommend you to switch to the processing rules. They're  the successors of the posting rules, and more easy to work with. 

Perhaps, you'd like to set up your new client with the processing rules immediately now, so you don't need to switch in the future? Just a recommendation. 🙃

For more information about processing rules, see: 

  • Processing Rules
  • Manage Processing Rules - For Bank Statements
  • How to Create Processing Rules
  • Overview on Automatic Bank Statement Processing

Best regards,

Anita

entry.title: Re: How to Derive the Duration in Years between 2 dates i...
entry.content:
Hi Ankit, 
that is an interesting use case. 
However, it is not fully clear what you want to achieve. If the target is to pick a price condition dependant on the year of a contract, then this is a valid approach. However, there are two observations:

First: the rounding rule of the duration component is defined as 'Round Down'. This means, that if the duration of the subscription start date to the billing cycle start date is below 1 year, then it is rounded down to 0.
So the result is Years = 0 years.
If you expected to get 1 year, then you would need to change it to Round Up.

Second: The years is also used as scale value and quantity. So in your example, you finally calculate the price for a quantity of 0 years, which is very likely 0 INR.
To me it looks like this is redundant. 
With the table lookup the quantity range already considers the calculated Years to identify the price condition.
Then probably you need to use the standard field 'quantity' in the Calculate statement.

Cheers,
Carsten

entry.title: Ariba Network
entry.content:
Ariba Network 为您提供与数千个供应商的单一集成点。同样，它为每个供应商提供与多个大型采购组织的单一集成点。您可以使用 Ariba Network 查找供应商以从其购买产品或服务，并邀请供应商建立贸易关系。在供应商接受邀请后，您可以通过 Ariba Network 向他们发送采购订单。

供应商接收采购订单并退回发票等。每个供应商都有一个 Ariba Network 帐户，可以指定其希望如何接收订单并生成订单确认、发货通知和发票等。

Ariba 为采购订单和发票业务场景提供 Data Mappings between Business application 和 Ariba Network 的 PI 标准交付内容。

entry.title: Manage Assignments with Assignment API in SAP Project and Resource Management
entry.content:
Overview

This blog post provides best practices guide on how to use the Assignment public API to perform CRUD (Create, Update, Read and Delete) operations for Assignments in resource management capability in SAP Project and Resource Management. It also includes instructions and examples on how to construct the REST call and build the payload for common scenarios. 

Setup/Configuration: You should follow the steps mentioned here to set up the API access. While accessing the API make sure you provide the Authorization: Bearer <access token>  as part of the Request Headers. 

Sections Covered:

  1. Maintain assignment with monthly distribution.
  2. Maintain assignment with weekly distribution.
  3. Maintain assignment with daily distribution.
  4. Delete assignment.
  5. Further information and resources. 

1. Maintain assignment with monthly distribution 

Use Case: Create a new assignment for a resource request with monthly granularity for more than one month.

As an example, let's create an assignment with staffed efforts of 100 hours each for the months May, June and July in year 2025.  

Best Practice: Make one call per assignment and provide details for each month in the payload of same call. Please find technical details and sample payload below.

Consider below details as an example and make sure to adapt to your needs. 

HTTP POST: <apiUrl>/AssignmentService/v1/Assignments

Request Body:

 

 

{
    "requestID": "00031745-860c-4b5c-864e-5c50a2a011c7",
    "resourceID": "1a33b6fe-8107-4b68-91fa-828029399fb5",
    "_monthlyAssignmentDistribution": [
        {
            "calendarMonth": "202505",
            "bookedCapacity": 100
        },
        {
            "calendarMonth": "202506",
            "bookedCapacity": 100
        },
        {
            "calendarMonth": "202507",
            "bookedCapacity": 100
        }
    ]
}

 

 

Response:

 

 

{
    "@context": "$metadata#Assignments(_monthlyAssignmentDistribution())/$entity",
    "@metadataEtag": "W/\"da5b296a49ea0d38879f6fb2a9cb5ace92b6d1b3098c9b387e2334a3f660f06b\"",
    "ID": "37eda2ff-971d-4e03-b4aa-1bcaaa13761a",
    "requestID": "00031745-860c-4b5c-864e-5c50a2a011c7",
    "resourceID": "1a33b6fe-8107-4b68-91fa-828029399fb5",
    "startDate": "2025-05-01",
    "endDate": "2025-07-20",
    "bookedCapacity": 300,
    "isSoftBooked": false,
    "_monthlyAssignmentDistribution": [
        {
            "assignmentID": "37eda2ff-971d-4e03-b4aa-1bcaaa13761a",
            "calendarMonth": "202505",
            "bookedCapacity": 100,
            "monthStartDate": "2025-05-01",
            "monthEndDate": "2025-05-31"
        },
        {
            "assignmentID": "37eda2ff-971d-4e03-b4aa-1bcaaa13761a",
            "calendarMonth": "202506",
            "bookedCapacity": 100,
            "monthStartDate": "2025-06-01",
            "monthEndDate": "2025-06-30"
        },
        {
            "assignmentID": "37eda2ff-971d-4e03-b4aa-1bcaaa13761a",
            "calendarMonth": "202507",
            "bookedCapacity": 100,
            "monthStartDate": "2025-07-01",
            "monthEndDate": "2025-07-31"
        }
    ]
}

 

 

Use Case: Update monthly assignment distribution of existing Assignment.

As an example, let’s execute the following updates to the assignment created above at once: 

  • Remove staffing for month 202505 (set bookedCapacity = 0 in payload) 
  • Update hours for month 202506 from 100 to 110 hours 
  • Create additional staffing for month 202508 for 120 hours 

Leave staffed hours for month 202507 same as before i.e. 100 hours.  

Best Practice: Make one call per assignment and provide details for only those months that need an update in the payload of same call. Please find technical details and sample payload below.

Consider below details as an example and make sure to adapt to your needs. 

HTTP PATCH: <apiUrl>/AssignmentService/v1/Assignments(37eda2ff-971d-4e03-b4aa-1bcaaa13761a)

Request Body:

 

 

{
    "_monthlyAssignmentDistribution": [
        {
            "calendarMonth": "202505",
            "bookedCapacity": 0
        },
        {
            "calendarMonth": "202506",
            "bookedCapacity": 110
        },                
        {
            "calendarMonth": "202508",
            "bookedCapacity": 120
        }
    ]
}

 

 

Response:

 

 

{
    "@context": "$metadata#Assignments/$entity",
    "@metadataEtag": "W/\"da5b296a49ea0d38879f6fb2a9cb5ace92b6d1b3098c9b387e2334a3f660f06b\"",
    "ID": "37eda2ff-971d-4e03-b4aa-1bcaaa13761a",
    "requestID": "00031745-860c-4b5c-864e-5c50a2a011c7",
    "resourceID": "1a33b6fe-8107-4b68-91fa-828029399fb5",
    "startDate": "2025-06-01",
    "endDate": "2025-08-20",
    "bookedCapacity": 330.00,
    "isSoftBooked": false
}

 

 

Use Case: Read monthly distribution of assignment.  

To read the final monthly distribution of the updated assignment, we can execute a read with the assignment ID while expanding the monthly assignment distribution as shown below: 

HTTP GET: <apiUrl>/AssignmentService/v1/Assignments(37eda2ff-971d-4e03-b4aa-1bcaaa13761a)?$expand=_monthlyAssignmentDistribution

Response:

 

 

{
    "@context": "$metadata#Assignments(_monthlyAssignmentDistribution())/$entity",
    "@metadataEtag": "W/\"da5b296a49ea0d38879f6fb2a9cb5ace92b6d1b3098c9b387e2334a3f660f06b\"",
    "ID": "37eda2ff-971d-4e03-b4aa-1bcaaa13761a",
    "requestID": "00031745-860c-4b5c-864e-5c50a2a011c7",
    "resourceID": "1a33b6fe-8107-4b68-91fa-828029399fb5",
    "startDate": "2025-06-01",
    "endDate": "2025-08-20",
    "bookedCapacity": 330.00,
    "isSoftBooked": false,
    "_monthlyAssignmentDistribution": [
        {
            "assignmentID": "37eda2ff-971d-4e03-b4aa-1bcaaa13761a",
            "calendarMonth": "202506",
            "bookedCapacity": 110,
            "monthStartDate": "2025-06-01",
            "monthEndDate": "2025-06-30"
        },
        {
            "assignmentID": "37eda2ff-971d-4e03-b4aa-1bcaaa13761a",
            "calendarMonth": "202507",
            "bookedCapacity": 100,
            "monthStartDate": "2025-07-01",
            "monthEndDate": "2025-07-31"
        },
        {
            "assignmentID": "37eda2ff-971d-4e03-b4aa-1bcaaa13761a",
            "calendarMonth": "202508",
            "bookedCapacity": 120,
            "monthStartDate": "2025-08-01",
            "monthEndDate": "2025-08-31"
        }
    ]
}

 

 

2. Maintain assignment with weekly distribution 

Use Case: Create a new assignment for a resource request with weekly granularity for more than one weeks.

As an example let's create an assignment with staffed efforts of 40 hours for each of the calendar weeks 202525, 202526, 202527. 

Best Practice: Make one call per assignment and provide details for each week in the payload of same call. Please find technical details and sample payload below.

Consider below details as an example and make sure to adapt to your needs. 

HTTP POST: <apiUrl>/AssignmentService/v1/Assignments

Request Body:

 

 

{
    "requestID": "00031745-860c-4b5c-864e-5c50a2a011c7",
    "resourceID": "1a33b6fe-8107-4b68-91fa-828029399fb5",
    "_weeklyAssignmentDistribution": [
        {
            "calendarWeek": "202525",
            "bookedCapacity": 40
        },
        {
            "calendarWeek": "202526",
            "bookedCapacity": 40
        },
        {
            "calendarWeek": "202527",
            "bookedCapacity": 40
        }
    ]
}

 

 

Response:

 

 

{
    "@context": "$metadata#Assignments(_weeklyAssignmentDistribution())/$entity",
    "@metadataEtag": "W/\"da5b296a49ea0d38879f6fb2a9cb5ace92b6d1b3098c9b387e2334a3f660f06b\"",
    "ID": "147f4fd5-20c2-4e3d-b716-a90a8d5795cf",
    "requestID": "00031745-860c-4b5c-864e-5c50a2a011c7",
    "resourceID": "1a33b6fe-8107-4b68-91fa-828029399fb5",
    "startDate": "2025-06-16",
    "endDate": "2025-07-06",
    "bookedCapacity": 120,
    "isSoftBooked": false,
    "_weeklyAssignmentDistribution": [
        {
            "assignmentID": "147f4fd5-20c2-4e3d-b716-a90a8d5795cf",
            "calendarWeek": "202525",
            "bookedCapacity": 40,
            "weekStartDate": "2025-06-16",
            "weekEndDate": "2025-06-22"
        },
        {
            "assignmentID": "147f4fd5-20c2-4e3d-b716-a90a8d5795cf",
            "calendarWeek": "202526",
            "bookedCapacity": 40,
            "weekStartDate": "2025-06-23",
            "weekEndDate": "2025-06-29"
        },
        {
            "assignmentID": "147f4fd5-20c2-4e3d-b716-a90a8d5795cf",
            "calendarWeek": "202527",
            "bookedCapacity": 40,
            "weekStartDate": "2025-06-30",
            "weekEndDate": "2025-07-06"
        }
    ]
}

 

 

Use Case: Update weekly distribution of existing assignment. As an example, let's execute the following updates to the assignment created above at once: 

  • remove weekly staffed hours for calendar week 202525 (set bookedCapacity = 0 in payload)
  • reduce staffed hours for calendar week 202526 from 40 to 30 hours
  • create staffing for calendar week 202528 for 40 hours 

Leave staffed hours for calendar week 202527 same as before i.e. 40 hours.

Best Practice: Make one call per assignment and provide details for only those weeks that need an update in the payload of same call. Please find technical details and sample payload below.

Consider below details as an example and make sure to adapt to your needs. 

HTTP PATCH: <apiUrl>/AssignmentService/v1/Assignments(147f4fd5-20c2-4e3d-b716-a90a8d5795cf)

Request Body:

 

 

{
    "_weeklyAssignmentDistribution": [
        {
            "calendarWeek": "202525",
            "bookedCapacity": 0
        },
        {
            "calendarWeek": "202526",
            "bookedCapacity": 30
        },
        {
            "calendarWeek": "202528",
            "bookedCapacity": 40
        }
    ]
}

 

 

Response:

 

 

{
    "@context": "$metadata#Assignments/$entity",
    "@metadataEtag": "W/\"da5b296a49ea0d38879f6fb2a9cb5ace92b6d1b3098c9b387e2334a3f660f06b\"",
    "ID": "147f4fd5-20c2-4e3d-b716-a90a8d5795cf",
    "requestID": "00031745-860c-4b5c-864e-5c50a2a011c7",
    "resourceID": "1a33b6fe-8107-4b68-91fa-828029399fb5",
    "startDate": "2025-06-23",
    "endDate": "2025-07-13",
    "bookedCapacity": 110.00,
    "isSoftBooked": false
}

 

 

Use Case: Read the weekly distribution details of the updated assignment. 

To read the final weekly distribution of the updated assignment, we can execute a read with the assignment ID while expanding the weekly assignment distribution as shown below: 

HTTP GET: <apiUrl>/AssignmentService/v1/Assignments(147f4fd5-20c2-4e3d-b716-a90a8d5795cf)?$expand=_weeklyAssignmentDistribution

Response:

 

 

{
    "@context": "$metadata#Assignments(_weeklyAssignmentDistribution())/$entity",
    "@metadataEtag": "W/\"da5b296a49ea0d38879f6fb2a9cb5ace92b6d1b3098c9b387e2334a3f660f06b\"",
    "ID": "147f4fd5-20c2-4e3d-b716-a90a8d5795cf",
    "requestID": "00031745-860c-4b5c-864e-5c50a2a011c7",
    "resourceID": "1a33b6fe-8107-4b68-91fa-828029399fb5",
    "startDate": "2025-06-23",
    "endDate": "2025-07-13",
    "bookedCapacity": 110.00,
    "isSoftBooked": false,
    "_weeklyAssignmentDistribution": [
        {
            "assignmentID": "147f4fd5-20c2-4e3d-b716-a90a8d5795cf",
            "calendarWeek": "202527",
            "bookedCapacity": 40,
            "weekStartDate": "2025-06-30",
            "weekEndDate": "2025-07-06"
        },
        {
            "assignmentID": "147f4fd5-20c2-4e3d-b716-a90a8d5795cf",
            "calendarWeek": "202526",
            "bookedCapacity": 30,
            "weekStartDate": "2025-06-23",
            "weekEndDate": "2025-06-29"
        },
        {
            "assignmentID": "147f4fd5-20c2-4e3d-b716-a90a8d5795cf",
            "calendarWeek": "202528",
            "bookedCapacity": 40,
            "weekStartDate": "2025-07-07",
            "weekEndDate": "2025-07-13"
        }
    ]
}

 

 

3. Maintain assignment with daily distribution 

Use Case: Create a new assignment for a resource request with daily granularity for more than one day.

As an example, let's create an assignment with staffed efforts of 8 hours for each of three days 5, 6, 7 in May, 2025. 

HTTP POST: <apiUrl>/AssignmentService/v1/Assignments

Request Body:

 

 

{
    "requestID": "00031745-860c-4b5c-864e-5c50a2a011c7",
    "resourceID": "1a33b6fe-8107-4b68-91fa-828029399fb5",
    "isSoftBooked": false,
    "_dailyAssignmentDistribution": [
        {
            "date": "2025-05-05",
            "bookedCapacity": 8
        },
        {
            "date": "2025-05-06",
            "bookedCapacity": 8
        },
        {
            "date": "2025-05-07",
            "bookedCapacity": 8
        }
    ]
}

 

 

Response:

 

 

{
    "@context": "$metadata#Assignments(_dailyAssignmentDistribution())/$entity",
    "@metadataEtag": "W/\"da5b296a49ea0d38879f6fb2a9cb5ace92b6d1b3098c9b387e2334a3f660f06b\"",
    "ID": "b457b28b-0076-47a7-b844-4ecf6ad534ed",
    "requestID": "00031745-860c-4b5c-864e-5c50a2a011c7",
    "resourceID": "1a33b6fe-8107-4b68-91fa-828029399fb5",
    "startDate": "2025-05-05",
    "endDate": "2025-05-07",
    "bookedCapacity": 24,
    "isSoftBooked": false,
    "_dailyAssignmentDistribution": [
        {
            "ID": "a7872fe7-4363-4208-b421-b7790b1c021e",
            "assignmentID": "b457b28b-0076-47a7-b844-4ecf6ad534ed",
            "date": "2025-05-05",
            "calendarWeek": "19",
            "calendarMonth": "5",
            "calendarYear": "2025",
            "bookedCapacity": 8
        },
        {
            "ID": "e853d06c-1f05-4cd3-84b9-c92853a0fb33",
            "assignmentID": "b457b28b-0076-47a7-b844-4ecf6ad534ed",
            "date": "2025-05-06",
            "calendarWeek": "19",
            "calendarMonth": "5",
            "calendarYear": "2025",
            "bookedCapacity": 8
        },
        {
            "ID": "e9cd8c42-71d6-4ab8-a018-8292706bb836",
            "assignmentID": "b457b28b-0076-47a7-b844-4ecf6ad534ed",
            "date": "2025-05-07",
            "calendarWeek": "19",
            "calendarMonth": "5",
            "calendarYear": "2025",
            "bookedCapacity": 8
        }
    ]
}

 

 

Use Case: Update daily distribution of existing assignment.

As an example, let's execute the following updates to the assignment created above at once: 

  • remove staffed hours for 2025-05-05 (set bookedCapacity = 0 in payload)
  • reduce staffed hours for 2025-05-07 from 8 to 4 hours
  • create staffed hours for 2025-05-08 for 8 hours 

Leave staffed hours for 2025-05-06 same as before i.e. 8 hours. 

Best Practice: Make one call per assignment and provide details for only those days that need an update in the payload of same call. Please find technical details and sample payload below.

Consider below details as an example and make sure to adapt to your needs. 

HTTP PATCH: <apiUrl>/AssignmentService/v1/Assignments(b457b28b-0076-47a7-b844-4ecf6ad534ed)

Request Body:

 

 

{
    "_dailyAssignmentDistribution": [
        {
            "date": "2025-05-05",
            "bookedCapacity": 0
        },
        {
            "date": "2025-05-07",
            "bookedCapacity": 4
        },
        {
            "date": "2025-05-08",
            "bookedCapacity": 8
        }
    ]
}

 

 

Response:

 

 

 

{
    "@context": "$metadata#Assignments/$entity",
    "@metadataEtag": "W/\"da5b296a49ea0d38879f6fb2a9cb5ace92b6d1b3098c9b387e2334a3f660f06b\"",
    "ID": "b457b28b-0076-47a7-b844-4ecf6ad534ed",
    "requestID": "00031745-860c-4b5c-864e-5c50a2a011c7",
    "resourceID": "1a33b6fe-8107-4b68-91fa-828029399fb5",
    "startDate": "2025-05-06",
    "endDate": "2025-05-08",
    "bookedCapacity": 20.00,
    "isSoftBooked": false
}

 

 

Use Case: Read the modified assignment with daily distribution information. 

To read the final daily distribution of the updated assignment, we can execute a read with the assignment ID while expanding the daily assignment distribution as shown below: 

HTTP GET: <apiUrl>/AssignmentService/v1/Assignments(b457b28b-0076-47a7-b844-4ecf6ad534ed)?$expand=_dailyAssignmentDistribution

Response:

 

 

{
    "@context": "$metadata#Assignments(_dailyAssignmentDistribution())/$entity",
    "@metadataEtag": "W/\"da5b296a49ea0d38879f6fb2a9cb5ace92b6d1b3098c9b387e2334a3f660f06b\"",
    "ID": "b457b28b-0076-47a7-b844-4ecf6ad534ed",
    "requestID": "00031745-860c-4b5c-864e-5c50a2a011c7",
    "resourceID": "1a33b6fe-8107-4b68-91fa-828029399fb5",
    "startDate": "2025-05-05",
    "endDate": "2025-05-08",
    "bookedCapacity": 20.00,
    "isSoftBooked": false,
    "_dailyAssignmentDistribution": [
        {
            "ID": "e853d06c-1f05-4cd3-84b9-c92853a0fb33",
            "assignmentID": "b457b28b-0076-47a7-b844-4ecf6ad534ed",
            "date": "2025-05-06",
            "calendarWeek": "202519",
            "calendarMonth": "202505",
            "calendarYear": "2025",
            "bookedCapacity": 8.00
        },
        {
            "ID": "96e760a0-2a57-4c22-b465-53785cf1819f",
            "assignmentID": "b457b28b-0076-47a7-b844-4ecf6ad534ed",
            "date": "2025-05-07",
            "calendarWeek": "202519",
            "calendarMonth": "202505",
            "calendarYear": "2025",
            "bookedCapacity": 4.00
        },
        {
            "ID": "ee9bf740-55c2-4c8c-9e64-b8dd49f1c34f",
            "assignmentID": "b457b28b-0076-47a7-b844-4ecf6ad534ed",
            "date": "2025-05-08",
            "calendarWeek": "202519",
            "calendarMonth": "202505",
            "calendarYear": "2025",
            "bookedCapacity": 8.00
        }
    ]
}

 

 

4. Delete assignment

Use case:  Delete the entire assignment along with its distribution.

As an example, the API call below will delete the assignment along with all the distribution (monthly/weekly/daily). 

HTTP DELETE: <apiUrl>/AssignmentService/v1/Assignments(089f55e2-fe6e-4575-bb1a-82c0bbe0a968)

Response Status: 204 No Content

5. Further information and resources

  • Assignment API in SAP Business API Hub: https://api.sap.com/api/Assignment/overview
  • Set up API access: https://help.sap.com/docs/PPRM_OD/a2ad92d065e04e8393d72cace620e0be/24b5d0bbc0c442a5a8d258f7c17cd393.html
  • API documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/PPRM_OD/a2ad92d065e04e8393d72cace620e0be/86847e59639c440b950ea5b920a07148.html

entry.title: Re: Story data dictionary (Not the old learning one)

entry.title: Re: MDK Binding and Control access issues
entry.content:
Unfortunately, it still doesn't work.

Rule (function import result):

            let actionResult = clientAPI.getActionResult("SearchArticle");     
            let articleData = actionResult.data;
            let pageProxy = clientAPI.getPageProxy();
            let pageClientData = pageProxy.getClientData();
            pageClientData.ArticleData = articleData;
 
Binding:

"KeyAndValues": [ { "Value": "{Description}", "_Type": "KeyValue.Type.Item", "_Name": "KeyValue0", "KeyName": "Description", "Visible": true } ], "MaxItemCount": 1, "_Type": "Section.Type.KeyValue", "Target": "#Page:Main/#ClientData/ArticleData", "_Name": "SectionKeyValue0", "Visible": true,

No option to redraw SectionKeyValue0.

PS. Is GET Function Import not supported by MDK? I can use POST only.

entry.title: Re: Solution Order Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content: @Anna_CG Thanks for the comment. Whilst enterprise project support is on the radar, there are no concrete plans to support this in the coming releases. At the moment, the focus will remain on customer projects. Good luck with the implementation and feel free to reach out with any further questions!

entry.title: Re: In-House Repair in S/4HANA Service - Start with an Overview
entry.content:
Hi Sambitr,
not sure, what exactly you did to get this error.

entry.title: Transition from SAP BYDESIGN TO S/4HANA PUBLIC CLOUD - Accelerator Adoption
entry.content:
Introduction

Data migration is a crucial process that ensures a seamless transition from a legacy system to a new system. The primary goal is to maintain business continuity while ensuring user acceptance of the new environment. The success of data migration depends on selecting the right method and flow for efficiently transferring data from source to destination.

In this specific case, the challenge was migrating from an existing SAP Business ByDesign system to an S/4HANA system. The following sections outline our general approach and provide key insights from the project. Fortunately, SAP provides tools and active support for this process.

Selecting the Migration Flow

When planning a data migration, it is essential to determine the right sequence and approach. Key aspects include:

  • Understanding business data and its dependencies
  • Defining a structured migration plan
  • Ensuring data integrity and transformation accuracy
  • Prioritizing critical business processes

During the project, we had to ensure that revenue processes continued to function smoothly after migration. This is always dependent on the company's industry. Key migration objects should be identified early and given the highest priority from the start. In this case, ensuring a clean migration of customer projects was the top priority. This naturally led to dependencies, such as customer master data, making priorities clear.

Since migration often involves a transformation of business processes, it is crucial to focus on the new target processes and derive the necessary migration steps accordingly.

Key Aspects of Migration

A successful migration requires careful planning and execution. The most important aspects include:

  • Data Extraction – Identifying source data and ensuring quality
  • Data Transformation – Adapting data structures as needed
  • Data Validation – Ensuring correctness and completeness
  • Data Transfer – Efficiently loading data into the target system
  • Testing and Reconciliation – Verifying data accuracy post-migration

Utilise SAP provided Accelerators

Accelerators are embedded in the Activate methodology, particular attention should be given to transition specific content, particularly the migration iFlows. The iFlows map and transform many of key master and transactional objects and can lead to significant timesaving, see these blogs for more details: Data Migration Accelerator: SAP Business ByDesign to S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition and Data Migration: SAP Business ByDesign to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition (XML & CSV)

Also do subscribe to SAP Business Accelerator Hub for more information on Migration Accelerators :

Discover Product Integrations | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Adoption and Expansion: IFlow Implementation

SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI) and IFlows play a significant role in data migration. Their benefits include:

  • Simplified integration between systems
  • Automated data transformation
  • Enhanced monitoring and error handling

Testing requires a robust system landscape, including:

  • Development and quality assurance environments
  • Simulation of real-world business scenarios
  • End-to-end testing to validate integration

Use Case: Master Data IFlow Challenges

In this project, the main modifications focused on the Message Mapping component within IFlow to ensure correct data transformation from SAP Business ByDesign to S/4HANA Public Cloud.

The main challenges included:

  • Understanding predefined IFlows developed by the SAP team
  • Handling complex data transformations
  • Debugging unexpected errors in Message Mapping

IFlow Utilization and Modifications

  • Focused primarily on modifying Message Mapping
  • Added filtering and scripting elements where necessary
  • Retained core artifacts while ensuring compliance with business requirements

Challenges and Solutions

Challenges:

  • Lack of initial familiarity with existing IFlows
  • Unexpected errors due to complex transformations
  • Efficiently handling large volumes of data

Solutions:

  • Close collaboration with the SAP team for guidance
  • Iterative testing and debugging
  • Leveraging best practices in CPI and Message Mapping

Conclusion

This project provided valuable learning experiences, particularly in working with existing IFlows within SAP CPI. The process was challenging but rewarding, emphasizing the importance of structured migration planning, collaboration, and continuous learning.

The successful modification of Message Mapping and ensuring data integrity led to a satisfying outcome, demonstrating the impact of effective integration strategies.

Since using CIM, we have invoices that have to be processed in the connected system in the GUI app ‘MIRO’ instead of in the Fiori apps ‘FB60’ and ‘F0859’, as was the case with the OpenText solution, because functions are required that the Fiori apps do not support. In our use case, Central Invoice Management is used by our accounting department and should be forwarded directly to the FI apps in the connected system. Even after intensive research, we still don't understand why these invoices now suddenly have to be processed via the ‘MIRO’ GUI app.

Is there a (technical) way to bypass the use of the GUI app ‘MIRO’, in case of doubt also with regard to our procurement process?

Many thanks in advance and best regards

Simon

SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Top 10 SAP Cloud ALM News February 2025
entry.content:
Welcome to our latest #SAP Cloud ALM update in February 2025! This month, we're thrilled to share a variety of new features designed to innovate and empower your project management, monitoring, and traceability capabilities. We’ll dive into the top 10 highlights from this February release to help you stay ahead in your digital transformation journey.

 

IMPLEMENTATION UPDATES

1. Automate and Schedule Transport Deployments

The new app in Deployment enables scheduling and automation of multi-feature deployment transport across your system landscape.

 

Find also more information in this blog post by @moritzgysler. 

 

2. Solution Process Viewer

When viewing solution activity details in the Solution Process Viewer, you can now see assigned applications and their types (e.g., SAP Fiori, Transaction, URL, SAP Web Dynpro). Custom applications also display the Display ID stored in the Library.

 

3. Assignment of applications

With the new Relations tab, you can now assign application library elements to test cases, enhancing overall test coverage and traceability in your implementation project; moreover, you can monitor the statuses of related test cases directly from the Libraries app.

 

4. Custom Solution Process Action History

You can now view a comprehensive history of actions performed on custom solution processes via an icon at the top of the process list. This history includes the action type, the user who performed it, and the timestamp, with the ability to switch between chronological and reverse chronological order, search, or filter by time range and action type (such as Created, Restored, Published, Created Draft, Edited, Archived and Deleted). Additionally, users can directly access the corresponding custom solution process from links within the history.

 

5. Enhanced Traceability in Solution Process Defects

You can now view both direct and indirect assignments in the Solution Process Traceability app. By clicking a bar chart in the Defects column, a popup displays directly assigned defects, with an option to enable the display of indirect assignments.

 

6. Role-Based Restrictions on Test Plan Actions

To prevent accidental loss of test results, only users with the roles of Project Administrator or Project Lead can delete test plans containing executed test cases or unassign such test cases from test plans. Project Members can still perform these actions on test plans and test cases that haven't been executed yet.

 

7. Display of Solution Activities in Configuration Details

Solution activities that were assigned to configurations are now displayed in the detail view, with links to navigate to the Solution Activity app.

 

 

OPERATIONS UPDATES

8. Health Monitoring Integration with SAP Datasphere

Health Monitoring now supports SAP Datasphere, providing metrics for Storage and Events.

 

 

9. Monitoring Enhancement for SAP Broker Reconciliation

Users can now track detailed statuses of incoming and outgoing messages for SAP Broker Reconciliation for Commodity Derivatives, and drill down to inspect individual issues and message details.

 

10. New Outbound Logs Section in Event Situation Details

The Event Situation Details page now includes an Outbound Logs section, allowing users to view the status, count, and timestamps of stored event payloads, particularly useful for events where the "Store Event Payload for 24 Hours" action is enabled. This feature also updates the Event Situations table on the Overview page with log counts.

 

Get inspired, stay empowered, and always keep innovating with SAP Cloud ALM. Let's make this your best month yet!

Stay tuned for more exciting updates and visit the SAP Help Portal!

So, are you ready to revolutionize your SAP Cloud ALM experience? Buckle up and get ready to explore the future of efficient, agile, and insight-driven project management. Let's make your digital transformation journey even more exciting with these top 10 highlights from our February 2025 release.

entry.title: Preliminary What’s New Information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.1
entry.content:
Preliminary versions of What’s New information and of the Product Assistance are now available for customers and partners to get a preview on the upcoming new and changed features of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.1.

You can find this preview on the current Help Portal page SAP S/4HANA Cloud (see What’s New section “Preview of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2502.1”). To access the Preliminary What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer or the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The RASD tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The preliminary information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2502.1 will be available in RASD within the next few days. We recommend using RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you. A valid S-User ID is needed to use the RASD tool and to view all the preliminary content stated above.

For any feedback you may have, try the Commenting Feature for What's New documentation in this release. See this blog for more information.

entry.title: Re: Subcontracting with chargeable components
entry.content:
It's better than "no support" as per note 912416 - Availability of Subcontracting with Chargeable Component

If a customer should choose to use SCC for subcontracting management of other countries, it must be understood that development support will be limited to bug fixing and any gaps found for those countries cannot be requested to be developed.

It means the support will be provided for technical issues (bugs) but not for missing functionalities required in countries other than Japan.

entry.title: Re: PR release cancellation - hierarchy
entry.content:
Hello @Elena12 

There is not such a concept as "un-approval" in purchase requisition strategies. An approver can either approve or reject. If an approver choses to reject, the requisition is fully rejected, it is not directed back to the previous approver. A rejected requisition can be updated, then a release strategy can be redetermined and started again.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Why does Hybris startup hang when near SessionFixatio...
entry.content:
After updating to version 2211.32, I encountered the same issue. Since I run Hybris in a WSL environment, I limited the number of assigned processors.

Simply add or modify the following lines in .wslconfig under %UserProfile%:

[wsl2]
# Number of logical processors assigned to the WSL 2 VM
processors=12

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Extended Warehouse Management Private Edition 2023 FPS03 – What’s New
entry.content:
We have recently released the latest updates for SAP EWM with the SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack 3 update. The enhancements of this release further improve our Advanced Shipping & Receiving integration and functionalities, complementing the features we delivered in the SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack 2 release. Furthermore, with this release we introduced a new Machine Learning based feature, called “Predictive Labor Demand Planning”, empowering shift managers at the warehouse to more accurately and efficiently plan out the tasks for the day. And to start today’s deep dive, we will begin with it.

PREDICITVE LABOR DEMAND PLANNING

With the SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS03 release, we deliver the new Machine-Learning based predictive labor demand planning (LDP) that can be used to predict planned workload data based on the picking and packing outbound processes. This enables warehouse shift managers and supervisors to plan resources and workloads more efficiently. Very summarily, LDP uses historical workload records to calculate a planned duration for warehouse workload records, and then use those records to plan outbound delivery orders.

 Historical workload records contain information about executed warehouse tasks (WTs) in the warehouse. They are not stored in an archive as all data is saved in the database, and outdated records are deleted. These records are only written for picking and packing tasks when a warehouse order is confirmed. These records contain application information that is mainly based on warehouse task data, with additional data from the assigned outbound delivery order.

The planned duration is a key component of a planned workload record. In labor management, you can use engineered labor standards to calculate the planned duration, but in labor demand planning, this is not necessary. Instead, labor demand planning uses historical workload records to calculate the planned duration for planned workload records.

Please note that if you have already set up engineered labor standards, they will still be used to calculate the planned duration. In this case, the system will not calculate based on historical workload records. You can also use engineered labor standards for one area of your warehouse and use calculations based on historical workload records for another area.

Outbound delivery order (ODO) processing includes picking and packing steps before the ODO item can be staged and loaded. You can use labor demand planning to get better visibility into the planned workload that is expected from outbound delivery orders. This allows you to predict peaks in the workload and balance the workload of the warehouse accordingly. The way LDP calculates ODO planned workloads is the following:

  1. The system searches for historical data that belongs to similar ODO items. The resulting data forms the basis of the calculations for the required data of the current ODO item.
  2. The system identifies the picking location, preferably by using rough bin determination.
  3. The system determines the external process step that is most used by similar ODO items.
  4. If similar ODO items require packing steps, the system calculates the packing steps based on the packing-picking ratio of the similar ODO items. The packing-picking ratio indicates how often the pick step of an outbound delivery order with similar attributes was followed by a pack step and is calculated from the historical workload data. Single pieces may need a pack step while whole pallet picks don’t need to be packed in addition.

 With LDP, it is possible to view planned workload durations directly in the warehouse monitor, allowing for better visibility and control over task planning. This functionality improves operational efficiency by streamlining the planning process and ensuring more accurate and timely execution of warehouse tasks as well as reduce inefficiencies by predicting the time required for tasks such as picking, packing, and outbound deliveries.

This is only a first step for Predictive Labor Demand Planning, and we plan to build on top of this feature with future releases. But we will reveal those steps at a future date.

ADVANCED SHIPPING & RECEIVING UPDATES

With the SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS03 release we continue to further extend the range of functionalities available with Advanced Shipping & Receiving as well as expand on existing ones. In the interest of time and convenience, I want to briefly touch upon every ASR update in one or two paragraphs, and we begin with integration to Global Trade Service:

  • Integration of Decentralized EWM and TM with GTS – This is a new integration of decentralized Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) and Transportation Management (TM) with SAP Global Trade Services (GTS) which supports the execution of the outbound, or export, process. It enables users to conduct compliance checks for freight units and freight orders and complete export declaration for consignments and freight orders.

  • Search for Advanced Shipping and Receiving Deliveries in Waves – In this release, we further build upon a feature we delivered in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, by enabling users to search for waves, wave items, and wave-relevant warehouse requests that are part of Advanced Shipping and Receiving deliveries in the Maintain Waves app (transaction /SCWM/WAVE) and the warehouse monitor (transaction /SCWM/MON).
  • Custom Checks for Loading Materials onto a Freight Order – We also provide BAdIs that add customer-specific checks for handling units or warehouse requests before loading execution in Advanced Shipping and Receiving. The checks take place during outbound delivery, and allow for the customer’s own logic for loading checks in:
    • The radio frequency (RF) screen before loading, where the system can show special warning or error message to the RF user
    • The App "Load or Unload Freight Orders", where the same or a similar BAdI is called when the loading execution function is used
    • The enterprise warehouse management (EWM) desktop transaction /SCWM/LOAD
  • TM Updates for Unpacked Products with Actual EWM Weight and Volume – With this update, when information regarding weight and volume for unpacked products in an outbound delivery order item is updated in Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), the relevant documents in Transportation Management (TM) are updated with the adjusted EWM weight and volume information. These updates are made after the “Ready for Shipping” status is set in EWM. The updated weight and volume are sent on product item level and on handling unit header level.

CONTINUOUS IMPROVEMENTS

To round of today’s overview, I would like to also go over some of the final topics that came to be as a result of the most recent Customer Connect program:

  • Add Short Text to Goods Issue and Cancellation - With this feature, it is now possible to provide a short text for goods issues and cancellations in several apps. A text of up to 50 characters can now be manually entered, for example, to add an explanation or other information to the goods movement in the following apps: “Manage Physical Stock – Products” (F5340); “Manage Warehouse Documents” (F5341). This text can be seen in the relevant material documents in other components of SAP S/4HANA using the “Display Material Document List” app. If synchronous goods movements are used from Inventory Management to Extended Warehouse Management (only available in EWM embedded in SAP S/4HANA), text can be entered in the “Post Goods Movement” app (transaction MIGO). The text is visible in the corresponding goods movement document in EWM, and can be seen in the monitor node at Documents -> Warehouse Task. This functionality has also been added to the consume and scrap actions in the “Warehouse Physical Stock by Product” API.

  • Scanning Alternative HU IDs During RF Processing - With this feature, it is now possible to scan alternative handling unit (HU) IDs on vendor labels which the system converts them to internal Extended Warehouse Management Handling Unit IDs. Converting the alternative HU IDs in RF transactions removes the need to re-label the HUs or create custom RF screens. Note that if multiple HU IDs are assigned to one alternative HU ID, the relevant internal HU ID can be selected.

FIND OUT MORE!

To see more details on the above mentioned topics as well as see what other enhancements we delivered with this release, I highly encourage you to explore our latest “SAP EWM in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS03 – What’s New” slide deck.

You can also find more technical details and documentation on the above listed topic and others we left out via the “What’s New” viewer on the Help Documentation portal.

For more information about SAP Extended Warehouse Management, please follow us on social media, our YouTube channel or our community pages:

SAP EWM Community

SAP YouTube Channel

EWM LinkedIn Community

SAP Extended Warehouse Management Roadmap Explorer

Release information and restrictions of embedded EWM on SAP S/4HANA 2023

Release information and restrictions of Decentralized EWM on SAP S/4HANA 2023

For a complete list of Q&A from the EWM community, please access this link:
https://answers.sap.com/tags/01200615320800000705

In case you do not find your specific question there, feel free to post your question via the following form:
https://answers.sap.com/questions/ask.html?primaryTagId=01200615320800000705

entry.title: Subcontracting in MTO with a purchase order or a service order?
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have a scenario in which we have a

  1. Sales order > Production order  with 2 operations.
  2. Operation 1 is happening in-house 
  3. Operation 2 (Semi-finished) material is sent to subcontractor for operation 
  4. Once operation is completed, material is sent back to us .

In Step 3, for sending a material to subcontractor, we have an external operation. Here we create a purchase requisition, which should be converted to a service or a purchase order? and in process subcontractor will receive a semi-finished material from us and consume it create a finished material, where that consumption and goods receipt is happening when it comes to SAP document? 

please help me to understand a standard process.

entry.title: Re: API_PROJECT_V3(Create multiple WBS nodes and Get Next...
entry.content:
To get the Next available number I used the same API API_PROJECT_V3 to get a list of Projects(ProjectExternalID) and based on that list I made a groovy code to assign the lowest free number to property NewProjectNumber

 

// Process Data
def Message processData(Message message) {
    def parser = new XmlParser()

    def body = message.getBody(java.lang.String);
    def root = parser.parseText(body)

    //Get the projectExternalIDs into a list
    def projectExternalIDs = root.'Project_Type'.'ProjectExternalID'.collect { it.text() }.sort()


    def nextNumber = 0

    //Loop through the projectExternalIDs
    for (ProjectID in projectExternalIDs) {

        //Get the number part of the external ID
        def numberPart = ProjectID.replaceAll("\\D", "")

        //If the nextNumber is 0(first pass on loop), set it to the numberPart
        if (nextNumber == 0)
            nextNumber = numberPart.toInteger()

        //If the numberPart is greater than the nextNumber, then we have found the next project
        if (numberPart.toInteger() > nextNumber) {

            def paddedNextNumber = String.format("%0" + numberPart.length() + "d", nextNumber)
            def nextProject = ProjectID.replaceAll("\\d*\$", "") + paddedNextNumber
            break
        } else

        //Possible next project
            nextNumber = numberPart.toInteger() + 1
        def paddedNextNumber = String.format("%0" + numberPart.length() + "d", nextNumber)
        nextProject = ProjectID.replaceAll("\\d*\$", "") + paddedNextNumber

    }

    message.setProperty("NewProjectNumber", nextProject);
    return message;

}

entry.title: service PO or a normal PO for a subcontracting in SAP MTO
entry.content:
Hi All,

In standard MTO we have a production order with an external operation to send the semi-finished material to a subcontrator, from the system point of view, in production order external operation, a purchase requisition will be generated. Here do we need a service purchase order or a normal purchase order? 

As a subcontractor will received a semi-finished material , perform a service and convert that to a finished material. 

Please suggest, shall we go with a service Po or a normal PO? 

As once operations / service is completed by the subcontractor, we will receive back the stock in our WH for packing etc.

entry.title: Re: Enhance MB21
entry.content:
Hello @taher123 

That is not possible without modifying MB21 substantially. 

Instead you can implement your own transaction, with a screen layout that you need and save reservations with the BAPI_RESERVATION_CREATE1 function.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Dynamic assignment variant for Field-symbols not allowed
entry.content:
Ciao Juwin.

The problem is that TEXT is not a structure so these suggested  variants cannot be used.

... { struc-(comp) } 
  | { dref->(comp_name) } 
  | { COMPONENT comp OF STRUCTURE struc } ...

I suggest to declare a proper structure like my_text and to put or duplicate the text symbol's values there as subcomponents.

entry.title: Re: Costs & Revenues getting mapped against separate WBS...
entry.content:
Hi @Ken_Melching 

I feel like Archimedes now, only I do not have a bathtub. Thank you. And thank you @RogerEarle, I figured out the method and then I saw your answer.

Solution: Right-click on WBS Element column and choose Select Hierarchy.

Just click Search and you will get an SAP default hierarchy for WBS. Then click OK.

Viola! We have the desired result, as answered by @RogerEarle. We can then just sort it by the ID, and also expand the hierarchy.

Regards,

Pratap.
